pattern: For every index used as the 'peak', the cheapest mountain through it pairs the smallest strictly-smaller value on its left with the smallest strictly-smaller value on its right.
hints: A mountain triplet is just three indices i < j < k where nums[j] is strictly greater than both its chosen neighbors; fix the middle index and ask what the best left and right partners are.
For a candidate peak at j, the cheapest valid left partner is the minimum value anywhere before j that is still less than nums[j], and symmetrically for the cheapest right partner after j.

Precompute, for every position, the minimum value to its left and the minimum value to its right; a position qualifies as a peak only if both of those minimums are strictly less than its own value, and its triplet sum is their total.

edge_inputs.description: mountain requires skipping over non-adjacent indices
strictly decreasing then increasing, no interior peak qualifies
minimum length of three, forms a valid mountain
minimum length of three, not a mountain
all equal values, no strict inequality holds anywhere
many candidate peaks, cheapest combination is not the first one found

stress.description: random small arrays near the max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 50))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
